steps.title: Definition of supplementary angles
steps.content: Two angles are supplementary when their measures add to 180 degrees. So if $x$ and $y$ are supplementary: $$x + y = 180$$

steps.title: Solve for $y$
steps.content: Subtract $x$ from both sides: $$y = 180 - x$$ This relationship MUST hold for any pair of supplementary angles.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• A) $y = 180 - x$ — this is exactly the rearrangement above, MUST be true.
  • B) $y > 26$ — not required (e.g., $x = 170, y = 10$ is supplementary but $y \lt 26$); eliminate.
  • C) $x = y$ — only true when both are 90; not required; eliminate.
  • D) $x + y > 180$ — supplementary means exactly 180, not greater; eliminate.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
